runs.context_blueprint: {"language": "objc", "build_system": "xcodebuild", "package_manager": "cocoapods", "build_commands": ["pod install --repo-update", "xcodebuild -project AwesomeMenu.xcodeproj -scheme AwesomeMenu -configuration Release"], "install_commands": ["apt-get update", "apt-get install -y git curl ca-certificates build-essential ruby-full ruby-dev", "gem install cocoapods"], "runtime_requirements": {"language_version": "N/A", "system_packages": ["git", "curl", "ca-certificates", "build-essential", "ruby-full", "ruby-dev"]}, "repo_type": "native_library", "base_image": "ubuntu:jammy-20260217", "base_image_rationale": "A stable, minimal Linux base to install Ruby, CocoaPods, and Xcode-compatible build tooling in environments where macOS is not available.", "pitfalls": ["iOS/macOS builds require Xcode on macOS; Linux containers cannot produce a real iOS app executable", "The podspec uses a git+SSH source (git@github.com:levey/AwesomeMenu.git) which requires SSH keys or credentials in the container", "Network access to GitHub is needed to fetch pods; SSH keys or authentication may be required", "Build commands assume macOS-like tooling (xcodebuild) which is not natively available on Linux"], "notes": "This blueprint provides steps to set up CocoaPods and attempt an iOS-style build in a Linux container. To actually compile and run the app, use a macOS environment with Xcode or a macOS-based CI."}
runs.final_dockerfile:
FROM ubuntu:jammy-20260217

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update \
 && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    git \
    curl \
    ca-certificates \
    build-essential \
    ruby-full \
    ruby-dev \
 && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# CocoaPods for pod management on Linux (best-effort; iOS/macOS builds require Xcode)
RUN gem install cocoapods

# Create a non-root user to avoid CocoaPods root restriction
RUN groupadd -r build && useradd -m -r -g build build

WORKDIR /src

# Copy the repository contents into the image
COPY . .

# Ensure proper permissions for non-root user
RUN chown -R build:build /src

# Switch to non-root user for subsequent operations
USER build

# Run a no-op pod install if a Podfile exists (best-effort in Linux)
RUN if [ -f Podfile ]; then pod install --repo-update; else echo "No Podfile found; skipping CocoaPods installation"; fi

CMD ["/bin/bash"]

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM ubuntu:jammy-20260217

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update \
 && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    git \
    curl \
    ca-certificates \
    build-essential \
    ruby-full \
    ruby-dev \
 && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install CocoaPods for pod management on Linux (note: this container cannot build iOS apps)
RUN gem install cocoapods

# Work in a dedicated source directory
WORKDIR /src

# Copy the repository contents into the image
COPY . .

# Attempt to initialize CocoaPods if a Podfile exists (Podspec alone is not enough to perform a pod install)
RUN if [ -f Podfile ]; then pod install --repo-update; else echo "No Podfile found; skipping CocoaPods installation"; fi

# Build a helpful note since Linux cannot natively build iOS apps with Xcode
RUN echo "Note: Building iOS apps requires macOS and Xcode. This Linux container sets up CocoaPods tooling but cannot perform xcodebuild." > /BUILD_NOTE.txt

CMD ["bash", "-lc", "cat /BUILD_NOTE.txt; echo; echo 'Container ready. For actual build, use macOS with Xcode.'; exec /bin/bash"]
"}

steps.tool_input:
{"path": "build_ios.sh", "content": "#!/bin/bash
set -e

echo "Starting iOS-like build in Linux container..."

if command -v xcodebuild >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  echo "Xcodebuild detected. This path would configure and build for iOS/macOS."
  if [ -f Podfile ]; then
    echo "Podfile detected. Running pod install --repo-update..."
    pod install --repo-update || true
  else
    echo "No Podfile found; skipping CocoaPods installation."
  fi
  echo "Attempting xcodebuild (will fail on Linux without macOS Xcode environment)..."
  xcodebuild -project AwesomeMenu.xcodeproj -scheme AwesomeMenu -configuration Release || true
  echo "Build step attempted. Note: On Linux, this will not produce a valid iOS app binary."
else
  echo "xcodebuild not found. Cannot build iOS app on Linux. Please run on macOS with Xcode for actual build."
fi

echo "Container ready. For actual build, use macOS with Xcode."

# Drop into a shell so the container remains interactive
exec bash
"}

{"path": "Dockerfile", "content": "FROM ubuntu:jammy-20260217

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update \
 && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    git \
    curl \
    ca-certificates \
    build-essential \
    ruby-full \
    ruby-dev \
 && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Install CocoaPods for pod management on Linux (note: this container cannot build iOS apps)
RUN gem install cocoapods

# Create a non-root user to satisfy CocoaPods' non-root requirement
RUN groupadd -r build && useradd -m -r -g build build

# Work in a dedicated source directory
WORKDIR /src

# Copy the repository contents into the image
COPY . .

# Ensure proper permissions for non-root user
RUN chown -R build:build /src

# Switch to non-root user for subsequent operations
USER build

# Attempt to initialize CocoaPods if a Podfile exists (will be skipped if none)
RUN if [ -f Podfile ]; then pod install --repo-update; else echo "No Podfile found; skipping CocoaPods installation"; fi

# Build a helpful note since Linux cannot natively build iOS apps with Xcode
# Note: Actual iOS/macOS build requires macOS with Xcode; this container prepares CocoaPods tooling only.
CMD ["bash"]
"}

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

CMD ["bash"]
"}

artifacts.content:
FROM ubuntu:jammy-20260217

ENV DEBIAN_FRONTEND=noninteractive

RUN apt-get update \
 && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    git \
    curl \
    ca-certificates \
    build-essential \
    ruby-full \
    ruby-dev \
 && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# CocoaPods for pod management on Linux (best-effort; iOS/macOS builds require Xcode)
RUN gem install cocoapods

# Create a non-root user to avoid CocoaPods root restriction
RUN groupadd -r build && useradd -m -r -g build build

WORKDIR /src

# Copy the repository contents into the image
COPY . .

# Ensure proper permissions for non-root user
RUN chown -R build:build /src

# Switch to non-root user for subsequent operations
USER build

# Run a no-op pod install if a Podfile exists (best-effort in Linux)
RUN if [ -f Podfile ]; then pod install --repo-update; else echo "No Podfile found; skipping CocoaPods installation"; fi

CMD ["/bin/bash"]
